w:r.w:t: CaféOS is a cloud-based restaurant operating system designed specifically for cafés and small-to-medium food establishments. The platform unifies every operational layer — from customer self-ordering via QR codes and staff-managed counter ordering, to real-time kitchen display, customer retention via CRM & loyalty, and owner-facing analytics — into a single, beautifully designed SaaS product.
The goal is to eliminate operational friction, reduce dependency on manual workflows, and provide the data-driven visibility that enables café owners to make confident decisions and scale profitably.
Fragmented café tech market dominated by legacy POS systems; high demand for affordable, modern SaaS alternatives
QR-first ordering, real-time KDS, built-in CRM & loyalty — all in one product with zero hardware dependency
Monthly/Annual SaaS subscription with per-outlet pricing and optional add-on modules
Network effects from CRM data, switching costs from embedded loyalty programs, multi-outlet lock-in

Most cafés and small food establishments currently operate with fragmented, manual, or paper-based systems. As order volumes grow, this leads to cascading failures in service quality and business performance.
Manual pad-and-pen taking; verbal relay to kitchen
Order errors, missed items, slow service
Verbal or paper tickets between counter and kitchen
No systematic tracking of who returns or why
Owner relies on manual end-of-day reports
Blind spots on peak hours, top items, staff performance
Cash counted at day end with no mode-wise breakdowns
Bottleneck; staff shortage directly impacts revenue
Manual spreadsheets or no system at all
Decisions based on intuition, not data
Digitize the end-to-end ordering process via QR and counter interfaces
Reduce average order processing time to under 2 minutes
Achieve > 95% order accuracy by eliminating verbal relay to kitchen
Provide real-time operational visibility to café owners via analytics dashboard
Enable multi-payment-mode recording and daily payment reconciliation
Build a recurring SaaS revenue stream with predictable MRR growth
Enable multi-outlet management from a single owner account
Become the primary operating system for cafés across India
Leverage CRM and loyalty data to build AI-powered insights in Phase 4
Business owner responsible for P&L, staff, and growth
Moderate — uses smartphone apps daily; comfortable with dashboards
Increase revenue, reduce manual overhead, improve customer retention, track daily performance
No operational visibility; manual end-of-day counting; unable to identify top/bottom menu items; staff accountability gaps
Sees real-time sales on phone; receives daily summary report automatically
Manages orders, billing, and payments at the counter
Low-moderate — needs simple, fast, tap-friendly UI
Process orders quickly, reduce billing errors, handle split payments without manual math
Manual calculations on paper; confusion when multiple tables have similar orders; no clear order status visibility
Can generate bill and record payment in under 60 seconds
Prepares food based on incoming orders
Low — needs large text, minimal taps, always-on display
See incoming orders clearly; know preparation priority; update status without leaving station
Verbal order miscommunication; unable to prioritize when multiple orders arrive; no preparation timer
New order appears on KDS screen within 5 seconds of being placed
Places orders via QR code at table or via counter/takeaway
Variable — system must work for first-time smartphone users
Order food quickly without waiting for a waiter; get accurate bill; settle payment easily
Waiting for waiter attention; ordering errors due to miscommunication; long queues at counter for bill
Completes order from QR scan to confirmation in under 2 minutes

CaféOS implements role-based access control (RBAC) across four primary user roles. Permissions are enforced at the API layer and reflected in the UI — unavailable modules are hidden, not just disabled.
CaféOS is structured into 15 modules, each with a distinct objective, feature set, business rules, and success criteria. Phase 1 (MVP) covers Modules A–J plus basic Analytics.
Allow café owners to register and configure their entire outlet — including tables, staff, and menu — within one guided onboarding session, without requiring any technical knowledge.
Café registration (name, address, GSTIN, logo, phone)
Café profile customization (theme, operating hours, social links)
Table count input with automatic table ID assignment
Automatic QR code generation for every table upon creation
Guided onboarding wizard with progress indicator
Downloadable QR package (ZIP with individual table PDFs)
Enters café details — name, address, GSTIN, logo
System auto-generates Table IDs (T01–T12) and unique QR codes
Owner downloads QR package (PDF or PNG, bulk or individual)
Lands on owner dashboard — setup complete
Minimum 1 table required to complete setup
QR codes are generated server-side; downloadable without internet at time of use
Café profile is editable at any time from Settings
Adding tables post-onboarding triggers new QR generation for the added tables only
Provide every table with a unique, scannable digital identity that ties customer sessions to specific tables and enables self-ordering without staff intervention.
Automatic QR generation on table creation
Individual QR download (PDF — print-ready A6 card with table name and café branding)
Individual QR download (PNG — for digital signage or custom printing)
Bulk download — entire café QR pack as ZIP
QR regeneration per table (with confirmation warning)
QR preview in dashboard before download
One unique QR per table; cannot be duplicated
QR code contains encrypted table ID + café ID — not guessable
Regenerating a QR immediately invalidates all prior QR codes for that table
Any active session on a regenerated QR is terminated with a user-visible error
QR codes do not expire unless regenerated
Ensure every customer interaction is tied to a verified mobile number, enabling CRM profile creation and loyalty tracking while keeping the authentication experience frictionless.
Mobile number input screen on QR scan
OTP verification and session token generation
Auto-login on subsequent visits within session validity window
Customer profile auto-creation on first verified login
OTP required on first access from any device
One active session per mobile number per table at any time
Customer cannot change mobile number mid-session
Blocked numbers (fraud/abuse list) receive no OTP and see a support message
Allow customers seated at a table to browse the full menu and place orders entirely through their smartphone, without waiting for a waiter.
Menu browsing with categories, item images, descriptions, prices
Item search and filter by category or dietary preference
Add to cart with quantity control and add-on/variant selection
Real-time order status tracking (New → Accepted → Preparing → Ready → Served)
Add more items to an active session (items merge into same table bill)
Orders are tied to the table's active session — cannot be assigned to another table post-placement
Multiple orders from same table merge into one consolidated bill
Customer can add new items as long as the table session is active
Unavailable items (marked Inactive/Future) are hidden from customer view
Customer cannot modify or cancel an order once it reaches 'Preparing' state
Allow counter staff to manually place orders on behalf of customers — walk-ins, phone orders, or customers who prefer not to use the QR flow.
Table selector with real-time occupancy status
Full menu access with same categories, items, and variants as QR flow
Customer mobile number capture (mandatory for CRM linkage)
Same order lifecycle as QR-placed orders
Quick reorder from customer's last visit
Counter orders require a valid table selection before items can be added
Counter orders for takeaway are handled in Module K, not here
Counter-placed and QR-placed orders for the same table merge into one bill
Operator must select or create customer profile before confirming order
Give counter staff and managers a real-time bird's-eye view of all tables — their occupancy, active orders, and billing status.
Visual table grid with colour-coded state indicators
Tap on any table to view active orders, customer, and order total
Table transfer — move all active orders from one table to another
Table release — only possible after bill is settled and marked 'Completed'
Pending order count badge on each occupied table
A table cannot be released while any order is in non-terminal state (New/Accepted/Preparing/Ready/Served)
Table transfer carries all associated orders and customer session to destination table
Closed tables reset to Available automatically after configurable grace period (default: 0 seconds)
Provide café owners and managers a full menu CRUD interface — with categories, items, variants, add-ons, images, and availability control.
Category creation with display order control
Item creation: name, description, price, image, category, availability
Item variants (e.g., Small / Medium / Large, Hot / Cold)
Add-ons (e.g., Extra Shot, Oat Milk) with independent pricing
Bulk availability toggle (mark entire category as unavailable)
Menu preview as customer sees it
Inactive items are hidden from all customer-facing views
Scheduled items become active automatically at the configured date/time
Deleting an item is a soft delete — it persists in historical order records
Image upload max: 5 MB, formats: JPG, PNG, WebP
Replace paper-based kitchen tickets with a real-time, tablet-optimised display that shows incoming orders, preparation timers, and status controls for kitchen staff.
Real-time ticket display — new orders appear within 5 seconds of placement
Ticket includes: table number, item list, modifiers, order time, elapsed timer
One-tap status updates: Received → Preparing → Ready
Audio alert on new ticket arrival (configurable)
Filter by station (e.g., Hot Beverages, Cold Items, Food) — Phase 2
Ticket history — view last 50 completed tickets
KDS operates on persistent WebSocket connection — auto-reconnects on drop
Ticket colour changes with time: green (0–5 min) → amber (5–10 min) → red (>10 min)
Marking a ticket 'Ready' triggers notification to counter staff
Kitchen staff cannot access billing, menu management, or CRM
Generate accurate, GST-compliant bills for dine-in and takeaway orders with support for discounts, split payments, and digital sharing.
Consolidated bill view for all orders under a table session
GST calculation (configurable rate per café; CGST + SGST breakdown)
Discount application — flat amount or percentage (with manager approval workflow)
Loyalty points redemption against bill total
Bill sharing via WhatsApp (formatted message with itemised list and totals)
Print-ready bill format (80mm thermal printer compatible)
Bill can only be generated when all items are in 'Served' or 'Completed' state (configurable override for owner)
Table cannot be released until bill status = Paid
Discounts above 20% require manager or owner role to apply
GST breakdown (CGST + SGST) is shown separately on bill if GSTIN is registered
Split payment totals must equal the bill total — system rejects mismatches
Capture granular payment data for every transaction — mode, amount, collected by, and timestamp — to enable accurate daily cash reconciliation and payment analytics.
Total payment for this mode (supports splits)
Staff ID of operator who recorded payment
Daily UPI, card, wallet collection totals
Support walk-in and website-based takeaway orders with a prepaid or counter-payment model and a trackable status flow.
Walk-in: Counter operator places order and assigns to takeaway queue
Website: Customer orders online (Phase 3 — Online Payments module)
All takeaway orders require payment before preparation begins (prepaid)
Takeaway orders appear on KDS with 'TKW' prefix for kitchen differentiation
Customer notified via SMS when order is 'Ready for Pickup'
Uncollected orders after 2 hours are auto-moved to 'Completed' with a flag
Build a growing customer database from every verified order interaction, enabling café owners to understand their customer base and identify retention opportunities.
Sum of all paid bills linked to profile
Timestamp of most recent completed session
Derived from order history — top 3 items
Customer list with search, filter, and sort
Individual customer profile view with full order history
Customer segments: New (1 visit), Regular (2–5 visits), Loyal (6+ visits)
Drive repeat visits and increase average order value through a transparent, easy-to-understand points-based loyalty program.
Linear — no bonus tier in MVP
Applied automatically if DOB is on profile
Birthday reward: auto-discount voucher generated 1 day before birthday
Milestone rewards: bonus points at 10th visit, 25th visit, etc. (configurable)
Membership tiers (Phase 2): Bronze / Silver / Gold based on annual spend
Enable café owners to manage staff accounts, track daily attendance, and monitor shift activity through the platform.
Staff account creation with role assignment (Counter Operator, Kitchen Staff, Manager)
Daily attendance log — login/logout timestamps auto-captured
Active session count per staff member
Payroll integration (TBD — integration with accounting tools)
Performance metrics per operator (orders processed, avg bill time)
Provide café owners with actionable, real-time and historical insights into revenue, operations, products, and customers — accessible from any device.
Customers with loyalty points ÷ total customers
Opens mobile web app; shows mobile input screen
Session created; customer profile created or loaded; menu displayed
Browses menu, adds items to cart
Cart updates in real time; item availability validated
Order created; KDS ticket sent; order status = 'New'
Bill generated with GST; loyalty points calculated
Adds items from menu; confirms order
Order sent to KDS; same lifecycle as QR order
Generates bill when food is served
Order created; assigned to takeaway queue
Order sent to KDS with 'TKW' prefix
SMS sent to customer; status = 'Ready for Pickup'
OTP-based for customers; email+password for staff
TLS 1.3 in transit; AES-256 at rest
Data model supports multiple outlets per account (Phase 3)
Mobile web (iOS Safari, Android Chrome); no app install required
10–15" tablet in landscape; works in Chrome kiosk mode
KDS shows last known state during connectivity loss; syncs on reconnect
Café Setup, QR Generation, QR Ordering, Counter Ordering, Table Management, KDS, Billing, Payment Tracking, Basic Analytics
CRM, Loyalty Program, Staff Management, WhatsApp Automations, Advanced Analytics
Loyalty enrollment > 30% in pilot cafés; NPS > 40
Inventory Management, Online Payments, Multi-Branch Support, Delivery Integrations (Swiggy/Zomato)
First multi-outlet customer live; online payment GMV > ₹5L/month
AI Insights Engine, Demand Forecasting, Dynamic Promotions, Franchise Dashboard
AI recommendations adopted by > 50% of owners; forecast accuracy > 80%
Before design and engineering begin, the following artifacts must be produced and signed off:
Wireframes — Customer, Counter, Kitchen, Owner flows
Acceptance Criteria for every feature (BDD format)
Kitchen Display System — tablet screen showing live order tickets to kitchen staff
Quick Response code — scannable code linking customer to a specific table and café
Customer Relationship Management — system for storing and analysing customer data
Role-Based Access Control — permission system based on user roles
Monthly Recurring Revenue — core SaaS business metric
Average Order Value — total revenue ÷ number of orders in a period
One-Time Password — 6-digit SMS code for customer verification
GST Identification Number — Indian tax registration number for cafés
Point of Sale — counter terminal used by operators to manage orders and billing
Document Exchange Unit — measurement unit used in Word document layout
All cafés operate within India — GST, UPI, and SMS OTP are standard assumptions
Customers have smartphones capable of scanning QR codes and opening web browsers
Café owners have access to a printer for initial QR code setup
Internet connectivity is available at the café (WiFi or mobile data)
Phase 1 does not require integration with any third-party POS hardware
Regulatory compliance (IT Act, DPDP Bill) to be reviewed before launch
Native iOS / Android apps (web-first approach in Phase 1)
Third-party delivery platform integrations — Swiggy, Zomato (Phase 3)
AI-powered menu recommendations and demand forecasting (Phase 4)
Which SMS gateway will be used for OTP delivery?
Will QR codes be printed in-house or via a print vendor?
What is the default GST rate? Will it be configurable per item?
Will loyalty points expire? If so, after how long?
Which cloud provider will host the platform? (AWS / GCP / Azure)
Will the platform support multiple languages? (Hindi, Regional)
